d_at":"2024-11-13T06:10:50Z","updated_at":"2024-12-06T00:53:13Z","closed_at":"2024-12-04T18:13:24Z","author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"### Describe the issue:

Hiya, I'm seeing an illegal instruction in function `DOUBLE_log_AVX512F` from `_multiarray_umath.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so` when running numpy 1.26.4 on a Knight's Landing CPU (aka Intel Xeon Phi). The problematic instruction is `vextractf64x2` -- according to [an internet reference](https://www.felixcloutier.com/x86/vextractf128:vextractf32x4:vextractf64x2:vextractf32x8:vextractf64x4) this falls under the `AVX512DQ` CPU feature flag which the KNL does _not_ advertise, so this could be an error in dynamic dispatch?

An older version of this workflow using numpy 1.24.2 succeeds. That implementation of `DOUBLE_log_AVX512F` only uses `vextractf64x4` and `vextractf128` from the VEXTRACT family.

It's worth noting that the workflow is running in a singularity container, however this doesn't affect the flags visible in `/proc/cpuinfo`¹, and the output of `numpy.show_runtime()` below seems to indicate that the machine is correctly detected as a KNL.

I don't see any explicit references to `vextract*` in the numpy codebase, so this could ultimately be a compiler issue. The numpy packages in use are from these images:

https://quay.io/repository/biocontainers/rnaquast/manifest/sha256:b1064d92b9c2cbf506902718e13325f08f6439a361a07fa46086175ced940a2a
https://quay.io/repository/biocontainers/rnaquast/manifest/sha256:917929562006aa015b8b529935ec345110155326df2950980e075089ec415edc

However I haven't found the build recipe for these numpy packages to compare the build environments.



¹

### Reproduce the code example:

```python
I don't have a self-contained example sorry
```


### Error message:

```shell
Program received signal SIGILL, Illegal instruction.
0x00002aaaac2c4f03 in DOUBLE_log_AVX512F ()
   from /usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/numpy/core/_multiarray_umath.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so


$ objdump -D /usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/numpy/core/_multiarray_umath.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so
...
00000000003b0430 <DOUBLE_log_AVX512F>:
...
  3b0ee5:       ff 15 c5 21 1e 00       callq  *0x1e21c5(%rip)        # 5930b0 <log@GLIBC_2.2.5>
  3b0eeb:       0f b6 8d 90 fe ff ff    movzbl -0x170(%rbp),%ecx
  3b0ef2:       62 f1 fd 48 28 9d d0    vmovapd -0x130(%rbp),%zmm3
  3b0ef9:       fe ff ff 
  3b0efc:       c5 fb 11 43 18          vmovsd %xmm0,0x18(%rbx)
  3b0f01:       eb 8f                   jmp    3b0e92 <DOUBLE_log_AVX512F+0xa62>
* 3b0f03:       62 f3 fd 48 19 d8 03    vextractf64x2 $0x3,%zmm3,%xmm0
  3b0f0a:       88 8d 90 fe ff ff       mov    %cl,-0x170(%rbp)
  3b0f10:       62 f1 fd 48 29 9d d0    vmovapd %zmm3,-0x130(%rbp)
  3b0f17:       fe ff ff 
  3b0f1a:       c5 f8 77                vzeroupper 
...

(note that the version of gdb I used to isolate the failure was too old to even decode the vextractf64x2 function, hence the use of objdump from a newer distro to get a dissassembly)
```


### Python and NumPy Versions:

```
>>> import sys, numpy; print(numpy.__version__); print(sys.version)
1.26.4
3.9.15 | packaged by conda-forge | (main, Nov 22 2022, 08:45:29) 
[GCC 10.4.0]
```

And from the older working image:

```
>>> import sys, numpy; print(numpy.__version__); print(sys.version)
1.24.2
3.9.15 | packaged by conda-forge | (main, Nov 22 2022, 08:45:29) 
[GCC 10.4.0]
```

            '08:45:29) \n'

Describe the issue:

Hiya, I'm seeing an illegal instruction in function `DOUBLE_log_AVX512F` from `_multiarray_umath.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so` when running numpy 1.26.4 on a Knight's Landing CPU (aka Intel Xeon Phi). The problematic instruction is `vextractf64x2` -- according to [an internet reference](https://www.felixcloutier.com/x86/vextractf128:vextractf32x4:vextractf64x2:vextractf32x8:vextractf64x4) this falls under the `AVX512DQ` CPU feature flag which the KNL does _not_ advertise, so this could be an error in dynamic dispatch?

An older version of this workflow using numpy 1.24.2 succeeds. That implementation of `DOUBLE_log_AVX512F` only uses `vextractf64x4` and `vextractf128` from the VEXTRACT family.

It's worth noting that the workflow is running in a singularity container, however this doesn't affect the flags visible in `/proc/cpuinfo`¹, and the output of `numpy.show_runtime()` below seems to indicate that the machine is correctly detected as a KNL.

I don't see any explicit references to `vextract*` in the numpy codebase, so this could ultimately be a compiler issue. The numpy packages in use are from these images:

https://quay.io/repository/biocontainers/rnaquast/manifest/sha256:b1064d92b9c2cbf506902718e13325f08f6439a361a07fa46086175ced940a2a
https://quay.io/repository/biocontainers/rnaquast/manifest/sha256:917929562006aa015b8b529935ec345110155326df2950980e075089ec415edc

However I haven't found the build recipe for these numpy packages to compare the build environments.



¹

### Error message:

```shell

(3) is the only distribution for which _multiarray_umath.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so contains `vextractf64x2` instructions within the `DOUBLE_log_AVX512F` function. Describe the issue:

Hiya, I'm seeing an illegal instruction in function `DOUBLE_log_AVX512F` from `_multiarray_umath.cpython-39-x86_64-linux-gnu.so` when running numpy 1.26.4 on a Knight's Landing CPU (aka Intel Xeon Phi). The problematic instruction is `vextractf64x2` -- according to [an internet reference](https://www.felixcloutier.com/x86/vextractf128:vextractf32x4:vextractf64x2:vextractf32x8:vextractf64x4) this falls under the `AVX512DQ` CPU feature flag which the KNL does _not_ advertise, so this could be an error in dynamic dispatch?

An older version of this workflow using numpy 1.24.2 succeeds. That implementation of `DOUBLE_log_AVX512F` only uses `vextractf64x4` and `vextractf128` from the VEXTRACT family.

It's worth noting that the workflow is running in a singularity container, however this doesn't affect the flags visible in `/proc/cpuinfo`¹, and the output of `numpy.show_runtime()` below seems to indicate that the machine is correctly detected as a KNL.

I don't see any explicit references to `vextract*` in the numpy codebase, so this could ultimately be a compiler issue. The numpy packages in use are from these images:

https://quay.io/repository/biocontainers/rnaquast/manifest/sha256:b1064d92b9c2cbf506902718e13325f08f6439a361a07fa46086175ced940a2a
https://quay.io/repository/biocontainers/rnaquast/manifest/sha256:917929562006aa015b8b529935ec345110155326df2950980e075089ec415edc

However I haven't found the build recipe for these numpy packages to compare the build environments.

However after spending a bunch of time with compiler explorer and pasting in the majority of `AVX512F_log_DOUBLE` code I've been unable to get it to generate _any_ `vextract` functions. I also note that the gcc compilers available there do not support `-march=common-avx512` which I believe is [used by the pip build](https://github.com/numpy/numpy/blob/b1e6ccd14b173b922f5d6a11bb252f4141dc42cd/numpy/distutils/ccompiler_opt.py#L391).

I don't see that flag in the meson.build files, which I believe conda-forge switched to [last year](https://github.com/conda-forge/numpy-feedstock/commit/64e547730646bffd710a7146b6ff17d3c4173fe0) -- may be related to the different code generation.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/numpy/numpy/issues/comments/2497219030/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
